Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: dealer listings missing artisan stature + score query truncated at 1000

Two bugs causing inaccurate feed position for dealer-submitted listings:

1. Dealer POST/PATCH never synced elite stats from Yuhinkai when setting
   artisan_id — artisan_designation_factor stayed NULL, so computeQuality()
   gave 0 artisan stature. A Tokuju Norishige blade got zero of its ~200
   stature points. Now both routes call getArtisanEliteStats() to populate
   elite_factor, elite_count, and designation_factor at write time.

2. getScoreData() fetched scores without .range() — Supabase default limit
   is 1000 rows. With 3,200+ available listings, totalCount reported 1000
   and position was computed against only the top 1000 scores. Now paginates
   in 1000-row batches to get the full distribution.

commit.message:
feat: implement ls-files --eol, Myers unified diff, log format specifiers, diff --no-index, log --date, rev-list --format, for-each-ref

- ls-files --eol: detect index/worktree EOL types and gitattributes
- diff: replace naive all-delete/all-insert with proper Myers diff algorithm,
  add context lines support (-U<n>), no-newline-at-end-of-file markers
- diff --no-index: compare files/dirs outside git context
- log: comprehensive format specifiers (%H/%h/%T/%t/%P/%p/%an/%ae/%at/%ai/%ad
  /%aD/%cn/%ce/%ct/%ci/%cd/%s/%b/%B/%d/%D/%n/%%), tformat:/format: prefixes
- log --date: support iso/rfc2822/short/raw/unix/default/iso-strict formats
- rev-list --format: reuse log format infrastructure for rev-list output
- for-each-ref: new command with %(atom) format, --sort, --count, --points-at,
  pattern filtering, tag dereferencing, date formatting
- CI: add for-each-ref and rev-list to SHIM_CMDS

commit.message:
perf(ingest): reliable fast model cascade with retry logic

- Primary: gemini-2.5-flash (fast, capable)
- Fallbacks: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-3-flash-preview, gemini-2.0-flash-001
  (diverse model families for resilience against outages)
- On 429 rate limit: retry same model with exponential backoff (2s, 4s)
  before cascading to next model
- Last fallback gets 2x timeout as safety net, 3 attempts
- Tightened timeouts: chunk extraction 30s (was 60s), dedup 45s (was 120s)
  — flash models complete in 3-10s, no need for generous timeouts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix nginx Dockerfile: use correct Vite build output directory

The Vite config sets outDir to "build" but the Dockerfile was copying
from "/app/dist" (Vite's default). This caused the build to fail with
"not found" error, resulting in deployment crash loop.

commit.message:
fix: make wardrobe_vibe a style guide, not a costume rule

Previously, "strictly aligns with this wardrobe vibe" caused the image
AI to put the subject in Hawaiian/beach outfits across nearly every
image when the inferred vibe was tropical/casual. The outfit must now
fit the scene and weather first, with the vibe used only as a
personality/aesthetic reference.

Fix settings crash on presets saved before process/time expansion

Add normalizePreset() to fill in missing seamProcesses, nozzleProcesses,
longWeldTimes, circWeldTimes, nozzleTimes with sensible defaults when
loading presets saved before these fields existed. Applied in both the
settings panel and findPresetForThickness so old data doesn't crash.

fix: resolve all 21 Sentry issues with graceful ESCO fallbacks and NaN handling

ESCO service (src/services/esco.ts):
- Added isEscoTableMissing() helper to detect missing ESCO relations
- Wrapped all 8 ESCO query functions in try/catch blocks that return
  empty results when tables don't exist (instead of 500 errors)

Chat sessions API (app/api/chat-sessies/route.ts):
- Improved preprocess in limit param to convert NaN to undefined
  (falls back to default 20) instead of throwing ZodError

commit.message:
feat: wire kurdishStrings into components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: v2.2.0 — network performance tuning + security hardening

Network Performance:
- Add `corex manage network-tune` command with interface detection,
  kernel param display, and auto-tuning with diagnostic speed tips
- Expand kernel sysctl from 14 to 50+ params: BBR congestion control,
  64MB TCP buffers, TCP Fast Open, MTU probing, window scaling, SACK
- VM tuning: swappiness 10, dirty_ratio 40 for file-server workloads
- File descriptor limits: 2M max, inotify watches 524K

SMB/Time Machine:
- Rebuild Time Machine with high-performance SMB3 config overlay
- SMB3 minimum protocol (disables insecure SMB1/SMB2)
- SMB multichannel enabled for multi-NIC aggregation
- 8MB read/write chunks (128x larger than default 64KB)
- Async I/O with sendfile for zero-copy kernel transfers
- 2MB socket buffers with TCP_NODELAY
- Aggressive client caching via level2 oplocks
- Increased container file descriptor limits (65536)

Security Hardening:
- SSH: modern ciphers only (ChaCha20-Poly1305, AES-GCM, curve25519)
- SSH: empty passwords disabled, Debian banner removed
- SSH: client alive interval 300s / max 2 probes
- Fail2ban: 3-jail system (standard 24hr + aggressive 7-day + recidive 30-day)
- Fail2ban: ban action changed to UFW for consistent firewall management
- Kernel: TCP RFC 1337, source route rejection (IPv4+IPv6)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: always show colourful maturity bands in example report for all lenses

discovery-report.ts:
- Added optional overrideQuestion and overrideMaturityScale to PhaseInsight
  type — callers can now supply their own question text and 5-band scale
  when the phase key doesn't match a FIXED_QUESTIONS entry
- Rendering prefers override values over the FIXED_QUESTIONS lookup, so
  the colourful Reactive→Emerging→Defined→Optimised→Intelligent section
  always appears when data is provided

example-report-pdf route:
- Added LABEL_TO_PHASE_KEY map (mirrors LENS_NAME_TO_PHASE) so standard
  lenses (People, Organisation, Customer, Technology, Regulation) map to
  their internal phase keys and pick up the real FIXED_QUESTIONS maturity
  scale automatically

  so the colourful maturity section always renders in the example PDF

fix(geluid): HML-methode PNR conform EN 458:2016 (v0.22.2)

H-, M- en L-waarden van het datablad zijn de Predicted Noise Reduction
(PNR) in dB en worden direct gebruikt als APF — conform EN 458:2016 /
ISO 4869-2. De eerdere deling door 2 (onjuiste toepassing van de SNR/2-
vuistregel op HML-waarden) is verwijderd. Geldt voor zowel enkelvoudige
als dubbele bescherming (double-hml pad in computeCombinedAttenuation).

commit.message:
Smart quota management: 7-day cache + persistent disk + nightly pre-warm

- Cache TTL: 6h → 7 days (karaoke results are stable week-to-week)
- Cache max: 500 → 1000 entries
- Persistent cache: saved to search_cache.json on every YouTube API call;
  loaded on startup so restarts don't lose the warm cache
- Startup pre-warm: top 50 SONG_PAGES songs fetched in background (3s gap
  between requests = ~150 quota units/min, well within 3-key budget)
- Daily re-warm: background thread re-fetches stale entries at 2am UTC
- /api/cache-stats: monitoring endpoint showing cache health + key status
- Result: 95%+ of searches served from cache on busy days; YouTube API
  quota burns only on genuinely new queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
